cluster_name: Italian court convicts 32 over 2018 Genoa Morandi bridge collapse
tldr: The headline layer presents near-unanimous crime-and-punishment framing: nine of twelve on-topic titles announce convictions and prison sentences, with 'sentenced,' 'jailed,' and 'condena' dominating across five languages. The Daily Mail uniquely foregrounds structural neglect ('not been fixed in 50 YEARS') in sensationalist all-caps, while Tagesschau is the only outlet whose headline surfaces victim ambivalence ('Gemischte Gefühle') rather than verdict outcomes alone.
event_summary: On 16 July 2026, an Italian court in Genoa convicted 32 people and sentenced former Autostrade per l'Italia CEO Giovanni Castellucci to 12 years in prison over the 2018 collapse of the Morandi motorway bridge, which killed 43.

evidence.quote: Top-Manager zu zwölf Jahren Haft verurteilt
condena ex-CEO a 12 anos de prisão
had not been fixed in 50 YEARS, court hears
Gemischte Gefühle nach Urteil gegen Top-Manager

silences.what_they_covered_instead: The sole Taipei Times headline ('Ex-Penghu council speaker receives final 7.5-year term for graft') is an unrelated Taiwan domestic corruption case caught by sentencing-vocabulary overlap; no headline from this bucket addresses the Genoa disaster.

single_outlet_findings.finding: The only headline to foreground structural neglect rather than judicial outcome: 'had not been fixed in 50 YEARS' (all-caps in original) places blame on chronic maintenance failure rather than individual culpability — a framing absent from all international wire and broadcaster headlines.

'Gemischte Gefühle nach Urteil gegen Top-Manager' (mixed feelings after verdict) is the only headline in the corpus to surface victim ambivalence at the homepage layer; every other outlet presents the verdict as an unambiguous outcome.

The word 'finally' in 'Highways company ASPI finally apologizes' signals delayed moral reckoning — a contrition framing unique to the Italian wire service, absent from all international headlines that focus on punishment rather than acknowledgment.
Noise headline within cluster: 'Jeweler convicted by top court of killing robbers' — an unrelated Italian Supreme Court ruling captured by sentencing-vocabulary overlap, not about the Genoa disaster.

Noise headline within cluster: 'Ex-Penghu council speaker receives final 7.5-year term for graft' — a Taiwanese domestic corruption case unrelated to Genoa, captured by surface-level court-sentencing vocabulary.

bottom_line: The headline layer reduces eight years of legal proceedings and 43 deaths to a single dominant signal — executives sentenced — with cross-language consensus from France, Germany, Brazil, Qatar, and the UK; the only headline-layer differentiation is the Daily Mail's sensationalist '50 YEARS' structural-neglect framing and Tagesschau's uniquely empathetic 'mixed feelings' title.
